Defendant Chiro-Medical Group, Inc.S Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Mujteba Naqvis Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Three
Off Calendar Aug-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHIRO-MEDICAL GROUP, INC., SUPREET SHAH, D.C.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff Mujteba Naqvis Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Three.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Chiro-Medical Group's Motion to Compel is GRANTED as to Request Nos. 41 and 45 for 2017 and 2018, and Request No. 43, and DENIED as to Request Nos. 41 and 45 for 2016. Plaintiff intentionally waived the tax return privilege for the years 2013, 2014, 2015, 2017, and 2018 by voluntarily producing integral parts of his tax returns for those years. (See generally Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial [the Rutter Guide] Ch. 8C-3, Sec 8:112-8:122, citing authority.) Neither the parties' briefs nor the record before the Court indicate Plaintiff waived the privilege for the year 2016. Weber ordered production for additional tax years without analysis and therefore is not persuasive; the tax return privilege is not so narrow that disclosure of any integral part of a return for a particular year operates as a waiver of privilege as to every year. Regarding Request No. 43, Plaintiff does not oppose the request. (See Opp. at p. 9.) The parties' respective requests for sanctions are DENIED.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
